_meta.description: SG-3 shared rule source of truth. Single canonical copy of the write-gate / validator rules (banned tics, dash detection, filler lexicon, repetition budgets, excluded aesthetic keywords, season-word ban, formula fixed positions). Loaded by BOTH the SG-3 Python write gate (tools/backfill_narratives.py via tools/sg_validation.py) AND the SG-4 Swift StyleGuideCoherenceValidator. Do NOT hand-duplicate these constants in either language; both load this file and a parity test asserts agreement. Provenance: docs/style_guide/style_standard.md (SG-0), docs/style_guide/decisions/profile_derivation.md (SG-1), tools/check_golden_guides.py (SG-0 golden checker).

dash.comment: Mirrors tools/content_audit_checks.py _EM_DASH_RE exactly. Matches em dash (U+2014), en dash (U+2013), and a bare double-hyphen used as a dash (not part of a longer --- run). Markdown '---' horizontal rules are exempt by the lookbehind/lookahead.

stamped_phrases.comment: Verbatim stock sentences that leaked from illustrative prompt text into the cache at scale (SG-3 run 2 audit: the Slate 'weight' compass appeared on ~85% of clusters incl. wrong-modality fire/air charts; 'a soft note used sparingly' in 118). BLOCKED everywhere (normalised, punctuation-insensitive) so every chart must phrase its compass/relief in fresh words. Append any new illustrative sentence a prompt introduces. The golden guides may use these; the cache at scale may not. SG-4: normalisation now expands contractions (contraction_replacements, applied to lowercased text before punctuation stripping) so 'doesn't' == 'does not', and `patterns` are regexes run on the normalised text to catch structural variants ('Weight and glitter do not') that bypassed SG-3's literal check.
phrases: Trust this physical instinct as your ultimate compass

american_spellings.comment: Blocked American spellings (word-boundary, case-insensitive). Base list from the SG-3 gate plus the SG-4 promotion of the gate blind spots hand-fixed in both caches (rigor, artifact, mold, pavement curb, pants, -ize verbs). allowed_phrases are stripped from the text before matching: 'curb chain' is a jewellery term (golden-legitimate) and 'track pants' appears in frost_ideal.md (the guides win). 'rigorous'/'rigorously' are correct British English and are NOT matched (word boundary on 'rigor' cannot match them).

concrete_lexicon.comment: Named concrete nouns (fibres, metals, stones, garments, colours) counted toward the concreteness floor (style_standard.md section 9). Placeholders also count (they resolve to named nouns at render). Warning-level: fewer than `minimum` named concrete nouns in a section. Moved verbatim from the sg_validation.py inline constant at SG-4 so the Swift StyleGuideCoherenceValidator loads the identical list.

banned_tics.comment: Case-insensitive substring match on user-facing prose. Folklore floor (retained regardless of frequency) + harvested list (>=~15% shipped-cache coverage, zero golden-guide occurrences). Append-mostly: removing a phrase requires a golden guide that uses it. Source: style_standard.md 7a/7b + check_golden_guides.py BANNED_TICS.

excluded_aesthetic_keywords.comment: Positive-assertion exclusions by register, plus a selfContained orientation extension. Mirrors ChartAestheticProfile.excludedKeywords (Swift) and profile_derivation.md section 9. Assertion-aware: Avoid/pass-over mentions are exempt; only positive assertions violate. 'statement' is deliberately NOT excluded for quietLuxury (Slate uses 'a statement belt' positively).

season_words.comment: Banned in palette_narrative prose (SG-2 decision 2e Layer B). The palette must describe resolved colours by role, never a season or seasonal-analysis label. Case-insensitive whole-word / phrase match. 'fall' is intentionally omitted from the bare list to avoid false positives on the verb; the seasonal-analysis compounds cover the noun sense.

filler_lexicon.comment: The 'substantial/expensive/heavy-class' budget. Counted as filler ONLY when used evaluatively; physical descriptors used physically (a heavy wool coat, a substantial buckle in a weight-test context) are the legitimate register and are not counted. Cap: <= 3 per section (warning level at SG-3). Source: style_standard.md section 6.

repetition_budgets.comment: Legitimate instructional phrases (the golden guides use them) that become tics at cache scale. Per-cluster budgets across all 16 sections combined, derived from golden maxima. Warning level. Source: style_standard.md section 8.

formula_fixed_positions.comment: coreFormula must appear at minimum at these 5 fixed positions (>=5 occurrences). Checked on composed output at SG-4; at SG-3 the write gate requires formula keyword presence in the cache keys that roll up to these positions (style_core, occasions_*, accessory_*). Source: style_standard.md section 6 + golden README decision #3.

write_gate.comment: Which checks BLOCK a write (error) vs merely WARN (write + triage-tag) in the SG-3 blocking write gate. Element-contract conformance is NOT machine-checked at SG-3 (manual rubric review at the human stop); the full element-presence contract is an SG-4 gate.
